t_ch: 你们怎么盯着我看，怎么样了，降灵术成功了吗？
t_en: Why are all of you staring at me? What happened? Did the seance succeed?

t_ch: …可以说是成功了吧？
t_en: ...I reckon it's a success.

t_ch: …可以说是失败了吧？
t_en: ...I reckon it's a failure.
...Huh?

t_ch: 虽然不知道刚才那算不算成功，但现在肯定是失败了…公输师傅，「阿休」是什么？
t_en: Although I can't be sure whether we succeeded, now I'm certain it's a failure... Master Gongshu, what's A-Xiu?

t_ch: …老夫没想到会再次听到这个名字。
t_en: ...I never thought I'd hear this name once more.

t_ch: 别卖关子了。
t_en: Come on, don't keep me in suspense.

t_ch: 「阿休」是老夫没有完成的玩具，它从来就没在这世界上出现过…所以，老夫也不知道它具体长什么样子，换句话说，老夫甚至不知道它究竟是什么……
t_en: A-Xiu is a toy that I did not complete, thus it never came into existence in this world... That's why its form remains a mystery even to me. In fact, I'm uncertain of what it even is...

t_ch: 既然是未完成的玩具，完成了不就知道了吗？
t_en: Since it's an incomplete toy, why don't you complete it to find out?

t_ch: 可是老夫已经做不出来了……
t_en: But I can no longer make it...

t_ch: 我听说你喜欢玩各种游戏，不是吗？要不了多久，你就会因为懂得太多、玩得太多、以及想得太多，对游戏再也提不起兴趣。
t_en: I've heard you're quite an enthusiast for all sorts of games, aren't you? It's only a matter of time before you find yourself knowing too much, playing too often, and contemplating too deeply. That's usually when the spark for gaming fizzles out.

t_ch: 事到如今，我再也不能沉下心去做那些只有以前会热衷的事了。
t_en: Nowadays, I find it increasingly difficult to focus on the things that once ignited my passion.

t_ch: 别忘了阿休，你的好朋友。
t_en: Don't forget your best friend A-Xiu.

t_ch: 无论心理阻力究竟如何，这件事不就是「做与不做」吗？
t_en: Regardless of the psychological resistance you have, this is a matter decided by "whether you want to do this or not."

t_ch: 我…就算做出来，也不会是阿休了……
t_en: Even if... I were to craft it now, it wouldn't be A-Xiu...

t_ch: 我相信人是会变的，不过，每个人也有些「本性难移」的部分。刚才，你立刻就回想起了「阿休」这个名字，也许它并不是那么容易舍弃的东西。
t_en: I believe that people change, but there's always an immutable aspect within each individual. Just now, you immediately recognize the name A-Xiu. It serves as a testament to its unforgettable nature.

t_ch: 别忘了阿休，你的好朋友。
t_en: Don't forget your best friend A-Xiu.

t_ch: 银狼的投影就此消失，而公输师傅也不吭声地离开了。
t_en: Silver Wolf's holographic projection disappears, and Master Gongshu leaves the place silently.

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}，不知道你能理解公输师傅吗？我不太理解。<color=#dbc291ff>下次来工造司时，再问问他吧。</color>
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}, can you understand Master Gongshu? I can't. <color=#dbc291ff>Let's return to the Artisanship Commission and ask him about it again next time.</color>

t_ch: 你们终于来了…对了，三月七你回去之后没感到什么异常吧？
t_en: You two are finally back... Oh yes, did you feel anything abnormal after you returned home that day, March 7th?

t_ch: 没有…吧。不过最近我感觉好像有一种「我与机械融为一体」的感受，感觉自己的想法、意识、愿望可以延伸，甚至能远程控制……
t_en: I guess... not. But recently, I have had the feeling that "mechanical spirits and I are one." It's as if my thoughts, consciousness, and desires can be extended and even controlled remotely...

t_ch: 到这就可以了！这说明你没有什么异常。
t_en: Stop right there! This means you are completely normal.

t_ch: 老夫在这里站半天了，却无～人～发～现～呐～
t_en: I have been here the whole time, yet NOT A SINGLE SOUL deigned to acknowledge my presence...

t_ch: 早就发现了，等着你呢。
t_en: We noticed. We're just waiting for you.

t_ch: 公输师傅，「阿休」呢？
t_en: Master Gongshu, where's A-Xiu?

t_ch: 上次回去之后，老夫心血来潮，翻箱倒柜地找到了阿休的设计稿。
t_en: When I returned home that day, I impulsively began sifting through my cabinet, where I stumbled upon A-Xiu's design.

t_ch: 所以阿休是什么样子的？
t_en: So what does A-Xiu look like?

t_ch: 看到设计稿时，老夫才回想起来，原来阿休是只随处可见的机巧鸟。
t_en: Upon reviewing my blueprint, it finally dawned on me that A-Xiu is nothing more than a ubiquitous cycrane.

t_ch: 一直想做却没有做的，就只是只机巧鸟？
t_en: The thing you've long desired to make was just a cycrane?

t_ch: 最初老夫还只是个懵懵懂懂的学徒时，就想做出个厉害的机巧鸟，那时我有无数天马行空的想法，但终究没有将它实现出来——毕竟我不想让师父看见我玩物丧志。
t_en: In my early days as a naive apprentice, my ambition was to fashion an impressive cycrane. I harbored countless lofty ideas, yet in the end, I failed to realize any of them — for I dared not let my master catch me indulging in mere toys.

t_ch: 再后来，老夫变得忙了起来。一开始我压抑着「将它做出来」的想法，默默告诉自己，等忙过了这段时间就做…等真的忙过了，我已经想不起这股冲动了。
t_en: As time went on, I found myself consumed by other obligations. Initially, I pushed aside the notion of "get it made," reassuring myself that I would attend to it once I had more time... But by the time I finished my work, the desire to see it through had faded away entirely.

t_ch: 后来老夫成了工正，有了时间。每当我准备开始动手时，一个想法便会幽幽地出现——「我真的想做出一个玩具吗？」
t_en: Subsequently, as I transitioned into the role of an artisan, my schedule afforded me ample time. However, each time I geared up to commence work on it, a nagging question would stealthily surface in my mind — "Do I truly desire to craft a mere toy?"

t_ch: 随即，我便感到索然无味，无法继续了。
t_en: As a result, I lost all enthusiasm and found myself unable to proceed.

t_ch: 生活磨平了成年人的棱角啊…
t_en: The individualism of adulthood can be dulled by the weight of reality.

t_ch: 明明是随便就能做出的东西，却始终做不出来，哈哈。
t_en: It would be so simple to create, yet it took me such a long time. Haha.

t_ch: 欲望被压抑太久是会消失的。
t_en: Repressing desires over an extended period can eventually lead to their dissolution.

t_ch: 老夫曾经为了「阿休」的设计睡不着觉，现在却提不起劲…哈哈。
t_en: There was a time when I toiled day and night into crafting A-Xiu's blueprint. Yet now, nothing seems to ignite that same drive within me... Haha.

t_ch: 既然你们向老夫展示了我笃定的「不可能」，为了你们，老夫也应该再试一试。
t_en: Witnessing what you all have achieved and how you showed me what I once deemed "impossible" has prompted me to try again — for the sake of all of you.

t_ch: 公输师傅故弄玄虚地掏出了一只机巧鸟。
t_en: Master Gongshu dramatically pulls out a cycrane.

t_ch: 我放下了杂念只想「先完成它」。时隔多年，我的阿休终于做出来了。
t_en: I cast aside all distractions, dedicating myself solely to the task of "bringing it to completion." And at long last, after so many years, I have succeeded in crafting my A-Xiu.

t_ch: …大人还真是不容易呢。
t_en: ...Adulting is hard.

t_ch: 三月七小姐，你不想成为大人吗？
t_en: Miss March 7th, don't you want to be an adult?

t_ch: 我、我只是想象不出到自己变成大人究竟是什么样子，{NICKNAME}，你呢？
t_en: I—I just cannot imagine how I will be as an adult. What about you, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 真是永远都不想成为大人呀…
t_en: I don't want to be a grown-up ever...

t_ch: 想早一点成为受尊敬的大人…
t_en: I want to become a respectable adult soon...

t_ch: 老夫有几百岁了，当我完成它的一瞬间，我感觉自己只是个年纪大一点的孩子。大人与小孩，似乎不存在真正的分界线。
t_en: Despite my centuries of existence, upon finishing the toy, I found myself feeling akin to a teenager. It appears the boundary between adulthood and childhood is blurred, if not nonexistent.

t_ch: …好像能够理解。
t_en: ...I think I can understand that.

t_ch: 看来那具金人司阍的身躯里确实藏着一只机巧鸟的机械灵魂，可惜现在它飞走了。
t_en: So, cycrane's Mechanical Soul indeed once existed in the body of that Aurumaton Gatekeeper. Unfortunately, its essence flew away.

t_ch: 没想到银狼还是个诗人？
t_en: I never thought you were a bard.

t_ch: 想听我说得简单直白一些吗？我是说，我的稀有以太灵没了。
t_en: Do you need me to put it in simpler terms? I mean, my rare Aether Spirit is gone.

t_ch: 至少你可以复制「阿休」的以太灵留作纪念。
t_en: At least you can replicate A-Xiu's Aether Spirit as a keepsake.

t_ch: 哎，也算是有个保底奖励吧。
t_en: Well, I guess there's at least a guarantee in the pulls.

t_ch: 银狼复制了「阿休」以太灵，惊讶地发现它兼具机械和异形的特征。
t_en: Silver Wolf replicates A-Xiu's Aether Spirit and is surprised to discover that it has both Mechanical and Aberrant stats.

t_ch: 哦哦哦哦哦！稀有的双重属性，成功了？！那句仙舟俗语怎么说来着——「山重水复疑无路，稀有属性藏不住。」
t_en: Ohhhhhhhh! A rare double type! Does that mean it's a success!? What's that saying from Xianzhou — "In the mountain of doubt, no escape is found. Rare stats, they can't be bound."

t_ch: 什么仙舟俗语，一听就是你乱编的。
t_en: When did Xianzhou have such a saying? I'm sure you're just making it up.

t_ch: 哎，公输师傅完成了「阿休」，你找到了稀有以太灵，只有我还没有找到答案……
t_en: Great! Master Gongshu completed his A-Xiu, and you found your rare Aether Spirit. Only my answer remains unfounded...

t_ch: 还在想剑斗的那件事吗，别担心，我就是你的答案，你来找我，我会帮你解决问题的。
t_en: Are you still worrying about the sword showdown? Fret not, I'm your answer. Come and find me, and I'll give you your answer.

t_ch: 那就一言为定了，你得帮我啊！
t_en: That's a deal! You need to help me!

t_ch: 别担心，包在我身上好了。
t_en: Don't worry. Leave it all to me.

t_ch: 这不是青雀吗？我好像第一次见到青雀在认真工作！
t_en: Is that you, Qingque? I don't think I've ever seen you taking your work seriously before!

t_ch: 看来你们对我误会很深啊，我必须解释清楚，本人可是一直都有在认真工作的。只是偶尔会摸鱼那么几个时辰……
t_en: It seems you have greatly misunderstood me. I'll have you know that I have always taken my job extremely seriously. I just happen to slack off for a few hours every now and then...

t_ch: …你们二位是来太卜司内找太卜大人的吗？
t_en: ...Are you here at the Divination Commission to see the Master Diviner?

t_ch: 我们是来找符玄大人的。
t_en: We're here to see Master Fu Xuan.

t_ch: 真可惜，太卜大人去玉阙仙舟出差了。
t_en: What a shame. The Master Diviner is currently on a business trip to the Yuque Xianzhou.

t_ch: 没有太卜大人的太卜司，看上去有些寂寞啊。
t_en: The Divination Commission seems so lonely without the Master Diviner around.

t_ch: 不寂寞，因为还有数不清的工作陪着我……
t_en: I'm not lonely. Look at all this never-ending work I have to keep me company...

t_ch: 我们当然是来找你的呀。
t_en: We're here to see you, of course!

t_ch: 找我？我们先说好啊，闲聊可以，但要是有工作……
t_en: To see me? Let me be clear, I will happily chat, but if you've come to me with work...

t_ch: 免谈。
t_en: Out of the question.

t_ch: 你是懂我的。
t_en: You really do understand me.

t_ch: 等等，你们到太卜司来，不会是打算一边悠闲喝着星芋啵啵，一边观看社畜卜者勤劳工作吧？最后再顺手拍照发到网上，说什么这才是「罗浮仙舟的日常风景」……
t_en: Wait, you're not planning on kicking back and drinking startaro bubble tea while you watch us diviners work ourselves to the bone, are you? Then you'd make some ridiculous social media post about this being "just another day on the Xianzhou Luofu" or something, right?

t_ch: 青雀小姐，完全误会了！我是来太卜司书库寻找绝世剑谱的。
t_en: You've gotten the wrong idea, Miss Qingque! I came to the Divination Commission repository in search of an ultimate sword technique.

t_ch: 寻找剑谱？是什么意思呢……
t_en: You're looking for a sword technique? I don't know what you mean...

t_ch: 你将三月七与公司的好事之徒约战的事细细道来。
t_en: You elaborate on the matter of the upcoming duel between March 7th and that IPC busybody.

t_ch: 剑斗的事情我完全不懂，但你要是想找书，我确实可以帮忙。
t_en: I don't know a thing about swordfighting, but if it's a book you're after, I can be of some assistance.

t_ch: 那就麻烦你了，青雀。
t_en: I'm counting on you, Qingque.

t_ch: 等等，你知道规矩的。
t_en: Hold on. You know the rules.

t_ch: 难道说，你需要我们的帮忙？
t_en: You mean... You want us to help you with something?

t_ch: 正是，你太懂规矩了。
t_en: Precisely, I knew you knew how this works.

t_ch: 太卜大人前往玉阙仙舟面见爻光将军。但是她老人家「算无遗策」，已经帮我们把每日工作排到下个月了。
t_en: The Master Diviner has gone to the Yuque Xianzhou to meet with General Yao Guang face-to-face. However, she has already pre-planned our daily workload for the entire next month thanks to her "planning for every scenario."

t_ch: 屋漏偏逢连夜雨…你们知道丹鼎司的新任司鼎大人吧？她大手一挥，说是要把药王秘传那里缴来的禁书送至太卜司书库封存起来…无形之中，我的工作量又又又翻倍了。
t_en: As if that wasn't bad enough... You know the Alchemy Commission's new Cauldron Master, don't you? As soon as she started her position, she waved her hand and ordered that the forbidden books confiscated from the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus were to be brought to the Divination Commission repository for sealing... Before I knew it, I was absolutely buried in work.

t_ch: 听上去全是跟书有关的工作……
t_en: I get the feeling that all these jobs have something to do with books...

t_ch: 没错，看来聪明的三月小姐已经发现了——你们来太卜司寻找书，而我在太卜司整理书，太巧了！这简直是送上门来…不是，咱俩简直是全银河最好的工作搭子！
t_en: That's right. Our smart little March 7th has already figured it out, I see. You have come to the Divination Commission in search of a book, and I am in the process of organizing books here. Isn't that just perfect? It's almost as if I'm getting fre— I mean, we'll be the best partners the Cosmos has ever seen!

t_ch: 你上班有工资，我们没有…
t_en: You get paid to work. We don't...

t_ch: 三月七，喜欢换个地方上班吗？
t_en: March 7th, are you looking for a new job?

t_ch: 送上门的苦力是吧？
t_en: You were going to say free labor, weren't you?

t_ch: 哎，咱们几个认识这么久了，说话生分了啊。
t_en: Ah, we've known each other so long, and you still treat me like a stranger?

t_ch: 我怎么会让客人上班呢！你们俩当然是来微服私访，体验生活的嘛～
t_en: As if I could make a visitor work for me! You two would be here on an undercover visit to experience the daily life of the Divination Commission~

t_ch: {NICKNAME}我们走吧，可以问问其他卜者……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let's go. We can ask another diviner.

t_ch: 等等，我怎么会亏待你们二位呢！请放心，如果你们和我一起去整理书库，你们就有权限翻看太卜司藏书，要是翻到剑谱了，你们不就赚到了吗？
t_en: Wait! Of course I would not just take advantage of you two like that! Rest assured that if you come and help me arrange these books, then you will be given permission to look at all the volumes kept in the Divination Commission. There! If you happen to come across a book recording secret sword techniques, that's a win for you as well.

t_ch: 这么说也有道理。那么事不宜迟……
t_en: Well, when you put it like that... There's no time to waste!

t_ch: 等等，今天就到此为止吧，我要准时下班了。
t_en: Wait, let's leave it here for now, it's time for me to finish work.
But...

t_ch: 无论是太卜大人在的时候，还是不在的时候，咱都不能让太卜司传出「压榨劳动力」的传闻！下次你们记得在我上班的时候来找我哟！
t_en: It doesn't matter if the Master Diviner is here or not, we cannot have rumors about the Divination Commission using "exploited labor" getting out! Next time, remember to find me during work hours!

t_ch: 青雀迈着轻松愉快的步伐离开了太卜司。
t_en: Qingque happily leaves the Divination Commission with a skip in her step.

t_ch: …青雀肯定是去打帝垣琼玉牌了吧。<color=#dbc291ff>下次再来太卜司看看吧。</color>
t_en: ...She must be going to play celestial jade.... <color=#dbc291ff>Let's come and check out the Divination Commission again another time.</color>

t_ch: 太卜司书库收藏了关于云骑军武技的各式孤本、残本，涉猎繁多，无所不包。
t_en: The Divination Commission repository holds many sole copies and fragmented records on Cloud Knights martial arts techniques covering all sorts of topics.

t_ch: 三月七翻翻找找，忙碌到最后，算是小有收获。
t_en: March 7th browsed the materials intently and after a long period of searching, found something that could be of some use.

t_ch: 让我看看，你都找到了些什么好东西？
t_en: Let me see, what have you found there?

t_ch: 你看看这个…《却邪剑谱》，看保存情况这里只有残本，一看就是了不得的秘籍。
t_en: Look at this... Demon-Binding Blade Technique. It looks like some of this book is missing, but it's clearly some kind of super-powerful secret text!

t_ch: 你看看《却邪剑谱》卷首语，是不是写着什么「欲练此功，引刀自戕」？
t_en: Take a look at what it says at the beginning of this Demon-Binding Blade Technique. Does it say something like "If you wish to master this art, you must use your blade to take your own life"?

t_ch: 没错，这是什么意思呢？
t_en: It does, what does that mean?

t_ch: 这是说，你要是想要练习这门剑术，你必须先要自己伤害自己的身体。否则，学会这门剑术也会伤害你的身体……
t_en: It means that if you want to learn this school of swordsmanship, you have to harm your own body. Otherwise, learning this type of sword skill will cause you harm...

t_ch: 总之，就像工作一样，只要沾上，横竖都得伤身体。
t_en: In other words it's like work. As long as you are involved in it, it will harm your body.

t_ch: 这么邪门的剑谱它强吗？
t_en: Would such an evil sword art be strong?

t_ch: 根据西衍先生的话本来看，它很强。
t_en: According to Mr. Xiyan, it's very strong.

t_ch: 云骑的剑谱，怎么就跟说书的西衍先生有关了呢？
t_en: What has a Cloud Knight sword technique got to do with Mr. Xiyan the storyteller?

t_ch: 嗐，这不说明它的危险名声在外，都传得神之又神了。
t_en: Well, doesn't that mean that it is so dangerous that word of it has become legend?

t_ch: 感觉剑谱作者是有恶意的…
t_en: I feel like the scroll writer has bad intentions...

t_ch: 那倒也未必。
t_en: Not necessarily.

t_ch: 那这个呢…《九十九阳真诀》？
t_en: Well, what about this Ninety-Nine Yang Incantation?

t_ch: 啊…那个呀，我听过的。光看名字就明白这门武功至阳至刚。它能让练成之人肌肉虬结，身高八尺，腰围也是八尺，臂围也是八尺。谁也当不下这怪力一拳。
t_en: Oh... I've heard about that one. Just from the name, you can tell that this technique is the pinnacle of the Yang techniques. Using it grants the user amazing muscle development. Their height shoots to eight feet, their waist also grows to eight feet and their arm circumference does, too. No one can withstand a single blow from such a powerful physique.

t_ch: 噫…那练成后还是人吗？
t_en: Uhh... So, are you still human after you master this?

t_ch: 武功嘛…只管克敌制胜，哪管你练成后还是不是人形。
t_en: This is martial arts... All that matters is subduing the enemy and taking victory. Who cares if you're still human-shaped afterward?

t_ch: 别问，旷世神功都是有代价的。
t_en: Naturally all powerful techniques come with a cost.

t_ch: …我倒是一直想问问，到底一直是谁在定价？
t_en: ...I've always wanted to ask, who is it that sets these costs?

t_ch: 有没有什么安全的绝世剑谱呢？
t_en: Is there no such thing as a safe and powerful sword technique?

t_ch: …可要是完全没有任何代价，听上去就很廉价。
t_en: ...Well, if it has no cost, it sounds like it will be pretty worthless?

t_ch: 书库里收着的残篇多半是云骑军研发武道的失败品，不但有可能要配上类似药王秘传这般的禁忌药物改造身体，还得配合各种危险的武器……
t_en: The majority of the incomplete texts in the repository are failed examples of martial research and development. Not only do they require combinations with forbidden potions like those found in the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, some of them even require all sorts of dangerous weapons.

t_ch: 当然，前人对武学的开发也是在不断进步的，你瞧瞧这批注——
t_en: Of course, the martial research of our ancestors was always making progress, look at this note here...

t_ch: 「想要练成九十九阳神功，且保留人形，这是有可能的……」
t_en: "It is possible to master the Ninety-Nine Yang Incantation whilst still maintaining your human form..."

t_ch: 有可能？！看看这位武学高人怎么说！
t_en: It's possible!? I want to see what this master has to say!

t_ch: 「我确信已发现了一种奇妙的法子，可惜这里空白的地方太小，写不下。」
t_en: "I have indeed discovered a fascinating method. Unfortunately, I do not have enough space left on this paper, and it will not fit."

t_ch: 听起来这法子得是一篇论文！
t_en: Sounds like this technique is going to be a whole essay!

t_ch: 哼，一开始就别写，净卖关子！
t_en: Hmph. Then why write it in the first place? What a con!

t_ch: 哎，看来想找到一本适合本姑娘的绝世剑谱，还真是不太容易。
t_en: Ah, looks like finding a suitable ultimate sword technique for me is not going to be easy...

t_ch: 我看出来了，三月小姐想要克敌制胜，却害怕承担练武的风险…要我说，这些风险不承受也罢，毕竟大部分人只是想打赢一场架罢了，犯不着付出这么大的代价。
t_en: It is clear to me that you wish to subdue your enemies and take victory, yet you are scared to take on the risks of mastering a martial art... If you ask me, I don't think you absolutely have to accept these risks. After all, most people only fight to win an argument, I don't think that requires a huge cost?

t_ch: 幻戏故事里的大侠们只用苦练神功击败对手，故事就圆满结束了，现实哪有这么简单？
t_en: The heroes of immersia stories always rely on only intense training and great skills to defeat their enemies, then their stories end happily ever after. Since when was reality ever that simple?

t_ch: 不现实吗？
t_en: Is that not realistic?

t_ch: 仇恨、委屈、嫉妒、耻辱…人类有太多复杂的情绪，怎么可能用「你打败我，我打败你」的方式就轻易解决了呢？要是「打斗」能解决一切，现实就没那么多烦恼了。
t_en: Vengeance, grievances, envy, shame... Humans have many complicated emotions. Do you really think things in the real world are as simple as "I defeat you, you defeat me"? If "fighting" could resolve everything, there would be nowhere near as many worries in the real world.

t_ch: …现实好复杂哦。
t_en: ...Reality is so complicated.

t_ch: 毕竟我是个卜者，从一开始就不相信「剑斗胜利等于解决问题」。
t_en: I am a diviner after all, I have never believed that "winning a battle means that a problem is solved."

t_ch: 时间差不多了，今天就先忙到这里吧，下班下班！两位辛苦了，我们再抓个人去打牌吧！
t_en: It's getting late, let's leave it here for today. It's time to clock out! Good work you two, let's see if we can find someone to join us for a game of jade!

t_ch: 哎，今天应该不会有什么收获了。<color=#dbc291ff>下次再来太卜司时，再找找看吧。</color>
t_en: Ah, looks like we made no progress again today. <color=#dbc291ff>Let's have another look next time we're at the Divination Commission.</color>

t_ch: 哦，看来这就是小麻雀找来的两位帮手？
t_en: Oh, if it isn't the little sparrow's two little assistants?

t_ch: 灵砂大人怎么也在这里？
t_en: What are you doing here, Master Lingsha?

t_ch: 之前，妾身从丹士长玉络那里得知，建木灾乱期间丹鼎司丢失了不少禁书，找回来又费了不少功夫。丹鼎司本不是能收纳禁书的机构，于是我和太卜大人商量了一下……
t_en: I previously learned from Chief Alchemist Yuluo that many forbidden texts were lost during the Ambrosial Arbor disaster, and it took a lot of work to recover them. The Alchemy Commission is not an organization that should be used to store forbidden texts, so I came to discuss with the Master Diviner...

t_ch: 然后…禁书就变成了我的工作量。
t_en: And then... these forbidden texts became my job.

t_ch: 我好像帮忙找回过几本禁书…
t_en: I think I helped recover a few of these texts...

t_ch: 我听你提起过…好像是有些危险、又有些神奇的书？
t_en: I have heard you mention them before... I remember you were saying that some of them were quite dangerous and mysterious?

t_ch: 丹鼎司的禁书都是些什么书？
t_en: What kind of books are these Alchemy Commission forbidden texts?

t_ch: 本不该存在于世上的奇书，既藏着疯狂与恐惧，也藏着恩惠与力量。
t_en: They are remarkable texts that never should have existed in this world. Not only do they contain madness and fear, they also hold blessings and power.

t_ch: 简直就像是我们之前聊到的「绝世剑谱」一样。
t_en: They sound like the "ultimate sword techniques" that we were talking about earlier.

t_ch: 妾身不是甩手掌柜，给你这小小麻雀添了不少麻烦，今日我也会帮你一起整理。
t_en: I'm not the kind to let others do all the work. I have caused you a lot of trouble, little sparrow, let me help you with the organizing.

t_ch: 哇！谢谢灵砂大人！早知道领导这么体贴，当年我就该选丹鼎司而不是太卜司了。
t_en: Wow! Thank you, Master Lingsha! If I'd known you were such a kind leader, I'd have chosen to go to the Alchemy Commission over the Divination Commission any day!

t_ch: 哦？我这就给太卜大人打小报告。
t_en: Oh? I will have to report that to the Master Diviner.

t_ch: 别别别！早些整理完书目，我们四个凑一桌打牌好了。
t_en: No, no, please don't! Let's just get these books sorted, and we can all play a nice game of jade together.

t_ch: 忙碌一番后，时间悄然流逝，丹鼎司禁书的整理工作也接近了尾声。
t_en: As the group busied themselves and time passed silently, the job of organizing the Alchemy Commission forbidden texts neared its end.

t_ch: 这时，众人才注意到书库一角的三月七早已昏倒了过去，旁边散落着一本禁书。
t_en: It was at this moment that the group noticed that March 7th had been passed out in a corner of the repository for some time, with a forbidden text open by her side.

t_ch: 不好了，灵砂大人，你快看看三月七的情况。
t_en: Oh no, Master Lingsha! Please take a look at what's happened to March 7th!

t_ch: 这小姑娘定是不小心翻开了禁书。青雀你先把书阖上，妾身会准备好焚香将她唤醒。
t_en: This girl must have accidentally opened one of the forbidden texts. Qingque, you close the book up, and I will prepare an incense to bring her to her senses.

t_ch: 我、我这就去处理禁书！
t_en: I—I'll handle the forbidden text now!

t_ch: 灵砂大人，我可以帮上什么呢？
t_en: Master Lingsha, can I help at all?

t_ch: 你去将三月小姐扶坐起来，她陷入到禁书幻象之中，你要用声音试着唤醒她。
t_en: Go and sit Miss March up. She has slipped into an illusion caused by the forbidden text, you will have to try to call her back with your voice.

t_ch: 你将昏迷的三月七扶起来，她的嘴唇翕动似乎在说些什么，你凝神闭眼仔细倾听。
t_en: You lift the unconscious March 7th to a seated position. Her lips are moving slightly, as if she is trying to say something. So, you close your eyes and listen closely to hear what she is saying.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}……
t_en: {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 我在。
t_en: I'm here.

t_ch: 三月…
t_en: March...

t_ch: 剑已成冢…只余孤「剑魔」一人……
t_en: This blade is now my tomb... The "Blade Demon" remains here alone...

t_ch: 三月，那不是你的经历…
t_en: March, that never happened to you...

t_ch: 三月，你快醒醒啊！喂——
t_en: March, wake up! Hey!

t_ch: …千年茫茫…若梦，转瞬…即逝……
t_en: ...Thousands of years... they pass like a dream. Before we know it... we pass on...

t_ch: 这么高深的话不像你啊…
t_en: When did you start spouting wisdom like that...

t_ch: 三月，活下去——！
t_en: March! Hang in there!

t_ch: 还不至于啦？！
t_en: It's not that serious, is it!?

t_ch: 随后在灵砂的醒神香帮助下，你成功地唤醒了身陷幻象的三月七。
t_en: A moment later, you manage to awaken March from the illusion with the help of Lingsha's awakening incense.

t_ch: 看来三月姑娘终于醒过来了。
t_en: I see you're finally awake, Miss March.

t_ch: 抱歉，是我太马虎了，不该让你接触到那么危险的东西。
t_en: I'm so sorry, I should not have been so careless and let you come into contact with such dangerous objects!

t_ch: …我已经没事了，谢谢大家的关心。
t_en: ...I'm fine. Thank you all for your concern.

t_ch: 刚才那本禁书叫作《孤影剑谱》，难怪三月小姐会好奇……
t_en: That book was called the Solitary Shadow Sword Scroll, I can see why you would be curious, Miss March.

t_ch: 在书中，我即是剑魔，剑魔即是我…我曾经因持剑刺伤一名义士懊悔不已，追求武学奥义又被高超剑艺所困，枯坐在剑冢前三十年后郁郁而终。
t_en: In that book, I was the Blade Demon, and the Blade Demon was me... I once stabbed and wounded an honorable man in battle and could not escape my guilt. I both thirsted for more martial arts knowledge and felt trapped by my great sword skills. Finally, I sat meditating before the blade tomb before dying a tragic and miserable death.

t_ch: 仅用短短一两刻钟的时间，这体验有些太漫长了。
t_en: That seems like a long story for something that only took about half an hour?

t_ch: 不过翻阅至书尾，我只感觉才看了一半。所以我想，可不可以再读一遍……
t_en: Although, when I got to the end of the book, it feels like the story is only half-finished. So, I was wondering if I could read it again...

t_ch: 三月小姐，这本书真的很危险！
t_en: Miss March, this book is extremely dangerous!

t_ch: 我明白了。三月小姐，书中故事切莫过度回味，今日回去后，不作他想早早休息，<color=#dbc291ff>下次你来太卜司的时候，</color>我们再谈谈，如何？
t_en: I understand. Miss March, please do not dwell on the book's story for now. Head back home and just focus on getting an early night. Then, <color=#dbc291ff>next time you come to the Divination Commission,</color> we can discuss it again, okay?
Okay...

t_ch: 嗯，等你恢复了以后，妾身会问个清楚的。
t_en: Right. Once you're all recovered, I will get to the bottom of this.

t_ch: 三月小姐，你想要再读一遍《孤影剑谱》，能告诉我为什么吗？
t_en: Miss March, can you tell me why it is that you want to read the Solitary Shadow Sword Scroll again?

t_ch: 我、我…原本是想要寻得绝世剑谱，掌握一门绝技。不过阅读了一遍《孤影剑谱》之后，该怎么说呢，我想帮助他…我想帮助书里的那位剑魔。
t_en: I—I... originally just wanted to find a powerful sword technique and master an ultimate skill. But, after I read the Solitary Shadow Sword Scroll, I... How should I put it? I want to help him.... I want to help the Blade Demon in the book.

t_ch: 要怎么才能帮助书中的剑魔呢？
t_en: How would you help the Blade Demon?

t_ch: 我暂时没想到什么好方法……
t_en: I don't know right now...

t_ch: 放下助人情结，尊重他人命运。
t_en: You have to release this need to help others and leave them to their fate.

t_ch: 话虽然是这么说的……
t_en: You say that, but...

t_ch: 剑魔曾因一次意外失手，人生就此被分成两段，前半段他寂寂无闻，倒也快乐；而后半段他练得一身惊世骇俗的武功，却心事重重，并不快乐。
t_en: The Blade Demon made one mistake and his life was split in two. In the first half, he was a nobody, but he lived happily; but in the second half, he had mastered earth-shattering skill, yet was racked with anxiety and miserableness.

t_ch: …倒是很传统的故事。
t_en: ...It's quite a common story.

t_ch: 他将自己的武学奥义留在书中等待有缘人拾起，我总感觉他期待着我去做些什么……
t_en: He left his martial arts mastery in these pages waiting for a fated person to come along and pick it up. I just can't help but feel like he wants me to do something...

t_ch: 哎，你这小姑娘，想要拯救一个过去留在书中的故事？有意思，妾身可以帮助你，不过你得先告诉我你会怎么做，否则我是不会同意的。
t_en: Ah, so it is your intention to rescue this story that was left abandoned in the pages of a book, young lady? Interesting. I can assist you, but you must first tell me what it is that you intend to do, or I shall not agree.

t_ch: 三月，你能改变书中人物命运？
t_en: March, can you change the destiny of the man in the book?

t_ch: 可惜我只是个读者，不是作者。
t_en: Unfortunately I'm just the reader, not the author.

t_ch: 三月，你能穿越时间回到过去？
t_en: March, can you go back in time to that moment?

t_ch: 要是有这本事，我就不站在这里了。
t_en: If I could do that, I wouldn't be standing here.

t_ch: 为什么剑魔明明掌握了一身本领，却没法得到令人满足的幸福呢？
t_en: Why is it that the Blade Demon was able to become such a master and yet never attained the satisfaction of a happy life?

t_ch: 三月小姐，你还记得我们聊过的幻戏故事吧？这些故事总是「福祸相依」，用后来的「幸福」来弥补之前的「不幸」，就好像可以「正负为零」一样。
t_en: Miss March. You remember the immersia stories that we talked about before, don't you? These stories are always "a combination of blessings and tragedy," using "happiness" in later life to make up for "misfortune" that was experienced previously. It's like adding a positive to a negative to return to zero.

t_ch: 但是，我们都知道这样的想法只不过是一厢情愿……
t_en: However, we all know that beliefs like this are just blind optimism...

t_ch: 幸福是真的，不幸也是真的。
t_en: Blessings are real, and so is misfortune.

t_ch: 没错，即便剑魔掌握了惊世绝技，也不会改变过去的遗憾。
t_en: That's right. Even if the Blade Demon was able to master an ultimate technique, that would not change the fact that he regrets what he did in the past.

t_ch: 我这人有些小市民情结，尽管有时会憧憬大英雄，但我也不禁会想，是不是没有命悬一线、险象环生的经历更加适合自己。
t_en: I can be a bit of a philistine sometimes. Though I sometimes long for the life of a hero, I always think to myself, maybe I'm more suited to a life that isn't fraught with danger where I'm constantly surviving by the skin of my teeth?

t_ch: 比起「断掉一只手臂后，在剑冢前学会了无敌剑术」这种故事，我觉得还是「平平淡淡地过好了完整的一生」的故事是我想要的。
t_en: I think a story of "living a complete, yet unremarkable life" is far more appealing to me than "cutting someone's arm off and mastering a perfect sword technique before the blade tomb."

t_ch: …说不定剑魔也是这样想的，但是他已经回不去了。
t_en: ...Maybe that is what the Blade Demon wanted, too?

t_ch: 作为书中之人，他的故事早已成书，无法改变。不过幸好这并非命运，只是本书。
t_en: As the person in the book, his story is already written and cannot be changed. Fortunately, this is not destiny, it is just a book.

t_ch: 作者可管不了读者的「阅读顺序」——三月小姐，我知道书的另一半在哪里了，你从后向前读便是故事的下半！
t_en: Actually, the author has no power over "the order in which the reader reads their book"! Miss March, I know where the second half of this book is, try reading the second half of the book from the ending to the beginning instead!

t_ch: 作者已死，这是读者的权利！
t_en: The author is long dead, the reader has the right to read their story however they like!

t_ch: 真是狡猾又随便的解释啊…
t_en: Such a sly and half-baked solution...

t_ch: 灵砂大人，这就是拯救书中故事的方法！
t_en: Master Lingsha, this is how I can rescue their story!

t_ch: 有意思，我们试试看吧——
t_en: Interesting, let us give it a try...

t_ch: 在准备充分之后，三月七从故事结尾开始往前阅读禁书。
t_en: After making time to prepare herself, March 7th began reading the forbidden text from the end of the story.

t_ch: 书中幻象你不得而知，想必那一定是「绝世高手逐渐放弃自己一身本领的故事」。
t_en: Though you do not know the story inside the book, you imagine it must be "the story of a great master gradually giving up on his outstanding skills."

t_ch: 一段时间之后，三月七醒了过来，《孤影剑谱》悄然变成了尽是空白的纸页。
t_en: After some time, March 7th awakens and the Solitary Shadow Sword Scroll silently fades to blank, white parchment.

t_ch: 禁书改变了！青雀的诡辩成功了！剑魔…不对，那位平凡人想要的就是这样的结局。
t_en: The forbidden text changed! Qingque's convoluted theory was a success! That's exactly the ending that the Blade Demon... No, I mean the regular mortal wanted.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，这说明剑魔的内心其实也只是个小市民吧。
t_en: Oh, it's nothing special. It just means that the Blade Demon was a philistine like myself.

t_ch: 可惜，我在剑谱里掌握的本事也在阅读过程里全部还回去了，什么也没有留下。
t_en: Unfortunately, I lost all the skills that I learned from the sword scroll while I was reading it backwards, I didn't learn anything.

t_ch: 三月小姐，你将书从头读到尾，又从尾读到头，你并非一无所获，你收获了故事。
t_en: Miss March. You read the book from start to finish, then from finish to start. You did gain something, you gained a story.

t_ch: 嗯嗯…至少我现在明白了，一味追求实力，也不一定符合本姑娘的人设。
t_en: Hmm... At least now I understand that blindly pursuing power doesn't necessarily work for my character...

t_ch: …书库的整理有条不紊，这都得多亏了你们，你们有什么需要帮忙，记得向我开口哦！
t_en: ...The repository is organized beautifully, I must thank you all. If there is anything that you need in the future, please just let me know!

t_ch: 嗯嗯。
t_en: Hmm...

t_ch: 还真有啊，不过我要强调一下，我可不会什么剑斗哦！
t_en: You actually have something? I'll tell you again, I don't know anything about sword fighting!

t_ch: 别担心，青雀小姐懂这么多歪理，肯定能帮到我的。
t_en: Don't worry Miss Qingque. With that twisted logic of yours, I know you'll be able to help me!

t_ch: 你好啊，{NICKNAME}。虽然你不认识我，但我可是对你知根知底。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. You may not know me, but I know all about you.

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 先自我介绍一下，我叫伦道夫，是来自匹诺康尼的见习梦境治疗师，受训于爱德华医生。最近正在为摘掉头衔上的「见习」俩字而努力。
t_en: Allow me to introduce myself, my name is Randolph. I am a Penaconian Trainee Dreamscape Healer, training under Dr. Edward. Recently, I've been working hard to remove this "trainee" part from my title.

t_ch: 我从一些朋友那儿听说了你的事，我想咱们俩应该是颇为相似的。
t_en: I've heard about you from a number of friends in Penacony, and I believe that you and I are quite similar.

t_ch: 哪儿相似了？
t_en: How so?

t_ch: 你和我一样酷，还是一样能打？
t_en: Are you as cool as I am? Or as great a fighter?

t_ch: 不、不，我想你误会了…我说的相似仅仅基于兴趣爱好的层面而言。
t_en: No, no I think you misunderstand me... I just mean that we have interests in common.

t_ch: 咱们俩都是如假包换的「垃圾桶爱好者」。
t_en: You and I are both bona fide "trashcan enthusiasts."

t_ch: 看得出来，你也很有品味。
t_en: I could tell that you were a man of fine taste.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，很高兴能得到你的认同。
t_en: Haha, I am happy you approve.

t_ch: 咱们俩算是异父异母的…
t_en: Though we may have different parents, I guess that makes us...

t_ch: …{F#亲姐弟}{M#亲兄弟}，是吧？
t_en: ...{F#Siblings}{M#Brothers}, right?

t_ch: 饭可以乱吃，话不可以乱说…
t_en: What nonsense are you spouting...

t_ch: 不要害羞。如果发自内心喜爱一件事，而它又无害于人，那我们为什么不大大方方地承认呢？
t_en: There's no need to be embarrassed! If you truly love something and that love does not hurt anyone, why would you not celebrate it loud and proud?

t_ch: 我从不讳言，<size=32>我，超爱翻垃圾桶的</size>。
t_en: I make no secret of it, <size=32>I love rummaging through trashcans!</size>

t_ch: 在被人们遗弃的空间中寻索人们曾经存在过的证据——我觉得世界上没有比这更能满足人类好奇心的事情了。
t_en: Searching for evidence that people once existed in a deserted land... I don't think there is anything else that greater satisfies the curiosity of the heart.

t_ch: 所以我将我的职业和兴趣爱好做了个结合，来到了仙舟进行我的毕业研究。
t_en: So, I have combined this passion of mine with my path in life and come to Xianzhou to complete my graduate research.

t_ch: 研究仙舟的垃圾桶？
t_en: Researching Xianzhou's trashcans?

t_ch: 可以这么理解。但我的研究对象是「精神垃圾」。你应该听说过「忆质」这种东西吧？
t_en: You could put it that way. However, my research target is actually "psychological trash." I'm sure you've heard of memoria before, right?

t_ch: 梦境治疗怎么和垃圾桶沾边？
t_en: What has being a Dreamscape healer got to do with trashcans?

t_ch: 你应该听说过「忆质」这种东西吧？它时刻反映着人类的精神状况。我想做的便是翻找人们的「精神垃圾」。
t_en: I'm sure you've heard of "memoria" before, right? Memoria constantly reflects the psychological state of people. What I want to do is go trashcan diving into people's "psychological trash."

t_ch: 很遗憾，仙舟根本没有垃圾桶！
t_en: I'm sorry to tell you this, but there are no trashcans on the Xianzhou!

t_ch: 一个文明绝不可能不制造垃圾，正如人类的存在会不停弥散「忆质」。
t_en: It is not possible for a civilization to not create trash, just like a person will never stop emitting "memoria."

t_ch: 而我想做的就是研究仙舟人弥散的忆质。你可以理解为，我想研究「精神垃圾」。
t_en: What I want to do is research the memoria that the Xianzhou people emit. I guess you could say that I want to research "psychological trash."

t_ch: 简单来说，我需要人手去「拍摄」，或者说定位漂游在仙舟人群间的「离散忆质」。
t_en: Put simply, I need people to help me "photograph" — or should I say "locate" — the scattered memoria floating amongst the Xianzhou people.

t_ch: 可别小瞧这些微不足道的忆质，就像垃圾桶里的内容反映着城市生活的质量。离散忆质也反映了人类精神状态的种种迹象，拥有极佳的医学研究价值。
t_en: You mustn't underestimate the importance of such trace amounts of memoria. Much as the contents of a trashcan reflect the quality of life in a city, scattered memoria reflects various indicators of mental states in people. It has outstanding value for medical research.

t_ch: 伦道夫说罢，将手机里面的照片与视频展示给你。影像里最初只是寻常的街景和人流。但人流中有个熟悉又违和的身影。
t_en: Having finished his explanation, Randolph pulls out his phone to show you some pictures and videos. At first, the images just seem to show regular street scenes and interactions. However, there is a familiar and unexpected figure among the crowd.

t_ch: 垃圾桶！
t_en: A trashcan!

t_ch: 正是，人类无意义的精神碎片——忆质，游荡在街头巷尾。
t_en: Precisely. The undervalued psychological fragments of people — memoria, roaming the streets and alleys.

t_ch: 我开发了一个成像程序，让这些离散的忆质团块能呈现在你的相机镜头里，用的正是咱们俩最爱的形象。
t_en: I have developed an imaging program that makes these scattered memoria clumps visible to your camera lens, using the physical form we both love most.

t_ch: 垃圾桶？
t_en: Trashcans?

t_ch: 不必怀疑，这正是咱们俩最爱的形象。
t_en: There is no need for doubt. It is precisely the thing we love the most!

t_ch: 这些人类无意义的精神碎片——忆质，在街头巷尾。我开发了一个成像程序，让这些离散的忆质团块能呈现在你的相机镜头里。
t_en: These are the undervalued psychological fragments of people — memoria, roaming the streets and alleys. I have developed an imaging program that makes these scattered memoria clumps visible to your camera lens.

t_ch: 所以，{NICKNAME}，我们要做的工作就是这么轻松愉悦。拿起照相机在仙舟的街头巷尾转悠，找到「垃圾桶」，咔嚓将它们拍下来。
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, this is the simple and joyous task ahead of us. Pick up your camera, roam the streets and alleys of the Xianzhou, and snap up pictures of these "trashcans" for me!

t_ch: 一旦离散忆质标记完成，我就会展开捕捉工作，对仙舟人的忆质展开研究。
t_en: Once the marking for scattered memoria is complete, I will begin the capture work and carry out research on the memoria of the Xianzhou people.

t_ch: 这合法吗？
t_en: Is this legal?

t_ch: 当然合法！事实上，仙舟人并不在意这些「灵魂的头皮屑」，以至于他们根本没有为此立法——我的研究自然不会有人禁止。
t_en: Of course it's legal! The Xianzhou people don't give a hoot about this kind of "spiritual dandruff," there's no way they'd make legislation to protect it... So, there is no way my research would be forbidden.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，看我光说了半天，你要不要实际上手试试啊？
t_en: Sorry, {NICKNAME}, I've been talking your ear off all this time. Would you like to give it a try for yourself?

t_ch: 试试就试试！
t_en: I'll give it a shot!

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 好吧，我在这等你。
t_en: Alright, I shall wait for you here.

t_ch: 拍的不错，朋友。
t_en: Some fine photos, my friend.

t_ch: 我之前的助手不知染上了什么要命的怪癖，一端起相机就执着于研究构图光影和分镜。蹉跎了半天什么成果也没有。
t_en: My last assistant had an insatiable quirk. They'd spend so long fiddling with the composition and lighting that when they finally picked up the camera, they weren't able to capture anything!

t_ch: 在下次采样前，我去把拍到的这些离散忆质收容一下。先校验一下收容的可行性。
t_en: Before I send you out for more sampling, I'm going to try and store this scattered memoria. I need to test whether containment is possible.

t_ch: 那么{NICKNAME}，后续如果你有空了，记得来找我啊。
t_en: Alright, {NICKNAME}, if you have time again in the future, remember to come and find me.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来啦。今天要不要帮我拍些离散「忆质」啊？和之前一样，拍到它们的位置后发给我，回收的事就交给我来。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you came! Do you want to help me photograph some "memoria" today? As before, send their location to me once you get a picture and leave the containment work to me.

t_ch: 不得不说，有你帮忙之后，「在研究死线前完成课题」的理想便不是梦了。连我的胃疼都好了一半。
t_en: I have to say, finishing my research in time for my deadline is no longer a crazy pipe dream now that I have you to help me! My stomach was starting to hurt from the stress!

t_ch: 开始今日拍摄。
t_en: Let's begin today's shoot.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 好吧，如果还需要补拍，欢迎随时过来。
t_en: Alright. If you need to retake any pictures, you can come back anytime.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，多谢你了。有了这些照片，我去收容那些忆质就方便多了。
t_en: Thank you so much, {NICKNAME}. These pictures will make collecting and containing the memoria much easier.

t_ch: 离散忆质是什么？
t_en: What is scattered memoria?

t_ch: 我想想怎么跟你解释…你一定有喝过苏乐达吧？匹诺康尼的汽水饮料，我用它打个比方。
t_en: How should I explain this... I'm sure you must have tried SoulGlad before, right? It's Penacony's signature soda. I'll try and use that as a comparison.

t_ch: 就像放久了的汽水无论密封多好，都会从其中逸出气体。人类的思维活动也会产生微量的「忆质」。它们太稀薄了，稀薄到可以忽略不计。
t_en: No matter how tightly a soda bottle is sealed, some gas will leak out after the bottle has been lying around for too long. Similarly, human thoughts produce tiny amounts of "memoria," but they are so diluted that they are almost completely negligible.

t_ch: 长久以来，匹诺康尼的心理治疗师都喜欢深入梦境，研究密集的忆质。但我不这么认为——被人们抛下的垃圾并非毫无价值，只是人们不懂得如何利用它而已。
t_en: Penaconian therapists have long preferred going deep into the Dreamscape to research concentrated memoria. However, I don't believe in this practice. The trash that people cast aside still has value — people just don't know how to make use of it.

t_ch: 我打算从长生种的离散忆质开始研究，进一步拓展心理治疗。也许总有一天，我能破解仙舟人所谓魔阴身的秘密。
t_en: I plan to begin by studying the scattered memoria of long-life species to broaden the study of psychological therapy. Perhaps there will come a day when I will be able to unveil the secret behind what the Xianzhou people call the "mara-struck."

t_ch: 总之，{NICKNAME}，后续如果你有空了，记得来找我啊。
t_en: Either way, {NICKNAME}. If you have time in the future, remember to come back and see me again.

t_ch: 啊，{NICKNAME}你来的正好。我刚一胃疼，就想到了你，一想到你，你就来了。这也算是仙舟人说的「心有灵犀」了。
t_en: Ah, {NICKNAME}, perfect timing. I just got a stomach ache and thought of you. As soon as I thought of you, you appeared! It's like that Xianzhou saying: "Two hearts beating as one."

t_ch: 今天的拍照内容可就拜托你了！
t_en: I'm counting on you for more photos again today!

t_ch: 开始今日拍摄。
t_en: Let's begin today's shoot.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 好吧，如果还需要补拍，欢迎随时过来。
t_en: Alright. If you need to retake any pictures, you can come back anytime.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，多谢你了。有了这些照片，我去收容那些忆质就方便多了。
t_en: Thank you so much, {NICKNAME}. These pictures will make collecting and containing the memoria much easier.

t_ch: 为什么用金银铜色区别？
t_en: Why do you grade them bronze, silver, and gold?

t_ch: 你发现了吗？噗噗，被同好发现了自己的小心思，还真是令人高兴。
t_en: Oh, you noticed? Hehe, having a fellow enthusiast notice my train of thought makes me so happy.

t_ch: 从金色到铜色，越醒目的颜色代表着这些离散忆质的密集度越高。当你在街头疲惫游走了一天时，你能瞧见一只金光灿灿的垃圾桶，该是多么振奋人心的事情啊。
t_en: From gold to bronze... the brighter the color, the more concentrated the scattered memoria is. I figured that catching sight of a bright and shining gold trashcan after a long day of working yourself to the bone would be a wonderful feeling.

t_ch: 顺带一说，这些垃圾桶的素材是我从爱德华医生的梦泡里发现的，也不知他们从何而来，我就顺手拿来使用了。
t_en: I should mention that I found the materials for these trashcans in one of Dr. Edward's dream bubbles. I've got no idea where they came from. So, I just took them and used them for myself.

t_ch: 看来除我们之外，世界上还有许多和我们有着同样兴趣的朋友啊。
t_en: It seems there are more people out there that share our enthusiasm, my friend!

t_ch: 资产复用是吧？
t_en: You're repurposing assets?

t_ch: 我是学生，没那么多钱。能复用一下就复用咯。
t_en: I'm a student. I don't have much money. If I can repurpose them, then I will.

t_ch: 有没有可能这就是我留下的…？
t_en: You don't think they're the ones I left, right...

t_ch: 那可真是太巧了。看来咱们在此相遇受到了伟大的垃圾之王塔塔洛夫的庇佑。
t_en: Well, isn't this a coincidence! It seems our encounter has truly been blessed by the great Garbage King Tatalov!

t_ch: 你应该不会主张要用这些垃圾桶的形象收费吧，我只是个穷学生，没那么多钱。
t_en: You're not planning on charging for the use of these trashcans' images, are you? I'm a student. I really don't have much money...

t_ch: 总之，{NICKNAME}，如果你有空了，记得来找我啊。
t_en: Either way, {NICKNAME}. If you have time, remember to come back and see me again.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，来的刚好。我把拍到的数据发给了爱德华医生，他亲口说「看起来有点搞头」。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're just in time. I sent the recorded data to Dr. Edward. "This looks pretty good." Those were his own words.

t_ch: 这可是个好消息，这说明我之前花掉的预算有着落了！
t_en: This is great news! That means I managed to get the budget I spent back!

t_ch: 总之，今天的数据搜集就交给你了！
t_en: Anyway, I'm counting on you for data collection again today!

t_ch: 开始今日拍摄。
t_en: Let's begin today's shoot.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 好吧，如果还需要补拍，欢迎随时过来。
t_en: Alright. If you need to retake any pictures, you can come back anytime.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，多谢你了。有了这些照片，我去收容那些忆质就方便多了。
t_en: Thank you so much, {NICKNAME}. These pictures will make collecting and containing the memoria much easier.

t_ch: 你搜集这些离散忆质干嘛？
t_en: Why are you collecting all this scattered memoria?

t_ch: 当然是研究咯。我想通过离散忆质研究{RUBY_B#魔阴身}人格解体{RUBY_E#}的治疗方案。
t_en: For research, of course! I want to use my research on scattered memoria to find a treatment method for {RUBY_B#being mara-struck}de-humanization{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 我在图书馆查资料时发现了关于「魔阴身」的记载，就这样产生了好奇心。
t_en: I found records on being mara-struck when I was searching for materials in the library and grew curious.

t_ch: 这些年我一直在为这个课题奔忙，按照既有的资料，除去仙舟人的特殊生理外，他们的记忆与情绪因素也在魔阴狂症的发作上有着举足轻重的影响。
t_en: I've been working on this research topic for the past few years and according to the materials I have so far, it is not just the unique physiology of the Xianzhou people that plays a decisive role in the development of mara, but their memories and emotions as well.

t_ch: 如果能从这些情感垃圾里找出记忆层面的病因，那么开发治疗方案也就有路可寻了。
t_en: If I can find a cause for this illness in the psychological trash, then there is hope for the development of a cure.

t_ch: 估计爱德华医生也想不到，这可是我翻垃圾桶时获得的灵感呢。
t_en: I bet Dr. Edward never would have imagined that I got this inspiration while trashcan diving!

t_ch: 总之，{NICKNAME}，如果你有空了，记得来找我啊。
t_en: Either way, {NICKNAME}. If you have time, remember to come back and see me again.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来啦，之前的拍摄很成功，距离预期搜集数也快达标了。托你的福，我的胃疼也好了大半了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're here! The last shoot was extremely successful, and we're almost at our projected target sample number. Thanks to you, my stomach ache is almost gone, too!

t_ch: 眼下可能还需要麻烦你再帮我拍摄那些离散「忆质」。
t_en: I may have to ask you to help me go and photograph some more scattered memoria for now, though.

t_ch: 开始今日拍摄。
t_en: Let's begin today's shoot.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 好吧，如果还需要补拍，欢迎随时过来。
t_en: Alright. If you need to retake any pictures, you can come back anytime.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，多谢你了。有了这些照片，我去收容那些忆质就方便多了。
t_en: Thank you so much, {NICKNAME}. These pictures will make collecting and containing the memoria much easier.

t_ch: 忆质和魔阴身真的有关系么？
t_en: Does memoria really have a connection to being mara-struck?

t_ch: 如果换做我们刚认识的时候我会跟你说，「一定有关系」。
t_en: If this were back when we had just met, I would have told you: "Absolutely."

t_ch: 但老实说，我的真实答案是「不知道」。
t_en: But, if I'm truly honest, my answer is: "I don't know."

t_ch: 原来你也不知道啊？
t_en: You don't even know?

t_ch: 别这么说，科学可是个严谨的活儿，要大胆假设小心求证。我自己提出的假设，当然得自己想办法验证嘛。
t_en: Don't sound so disappointed. Science is a meticulous profession. One has to bravely set hypotheses and carefully confirm them. This is a hypothesis that I myself suggested, so naturally, it falls upon me to find a way to prove it.

t_ch: 我来罗浮仙舟也有一段时间了，对仙舟长生种的漫长生命算是略知一二。他们中许多人在同样的生活模式里循环往复，像是被困在琥珀里的标本。
t_en: I have been on the Xianzhou Luofu for some time now, and I have a rough understanding of the endless existences of long-life species. Many of them repeat the same life patterns over and over, like samples encased in amber.

t_ch: 虽然生活模式重复，但生活每天带来的烦恼与压力却千变万化。想象一下，你循规蹈矩地写了一百年公务文档，承受来自上级的压力和同事间的摩擦……
t_en: Although they repeat their ways of life, the worries and pressures of life come in endless forms. Imagine for a second that you have spent a hundred years working to write official documents, enduring pressure from superiors and friction with colleagues...

t_ch: 如此郁积的情绪迟早会把内心变成一个没有出口的垃圾桶，任由烦恼、恐惧之类的情绪填满却无处释放。
t_en: Sooner or later, these pent-up emotions will transform one's mind and heart into an enclosed trashcan — filled with emotions like worry and fear, with no chance of release.

t_ch: 我打算对捕捉的忆质进行分析，看看其中映射的情绪是否与魔阴身的病因有所关联——可惜，我还没有得到接触病人的许可，只能先在街巷里展开研究。
t_en: I plan to analyze the memoria we have collected and see if the emotions present within have a connection with being mara-struck or not... Unfortunately, I still lack the permission to meet with a mara-struck patient. So, all I can do for now is conduct research on the streets.

t_ch: 好了，感谢你听我唠叨了那么多。{NICKNAME}，如果你有空了，记得来找我啊。
t_en: Alright, thank you for listening to my rambling. {NICKNAME}, if you have spare time, remember to come and see me.

t_ch: 嘿，{NICKNAME}，告诉你个好消息！感谢你的帮忙，我的研究材料收集得差不多了。如果这项研究能成功，对仙舟人来说也是功德一件。
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, great news! Thanks to your help, my research data is almost complete. If this research is successful, it will be a huge help to the Xianzhou people.

t_ch: 至于你，我的朋友，你在这次的研究中居功至伟，我答应过的报酬绝不会少你的。
t_en: As for you, my friend, I would never deny you the payment I promised for your invaluable assistance.

t_ch: 总之先完成最后的拍摄采样吧！
t_en: But first, let's take care of the final photography session!

t_ch: 开始今日拍摄。
t_en: Let's begin today's shoot.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 好吧，如果还需要补拍，欢迎随时过来。
t_en: Alright. If you need to retake any pictures, you can come back anytime.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，真不知道该怎么谢你！数据搜集的差不多了！论文也要启动了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I really don't know how to thank you! The data collection is almost complete! It's time to get started on my thesis!

t_ch: 后续你有什么计划？
t_en: What are your plans from here?

t_ch: 我买了回匹诺康尼的船票，在登舰前还会在罗浮待上一段时间。你可以随时找我。
t_en: I've bought a ship ticket back to Penacony, but I will be around the Luofu for a while before I return. You can come and see me whenever you like.

t_ch: 接下来要做的便是在死线前永无休止的实验和论文了，也许很长一段时间我不能再翻垃圾桶了，真是遗憾啊。
t_en: From here it's just endless experiments and thesis writing until the deadline. It may be a long time until I can go trashcan diving again, which is a real shame.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，作为同好，你可要连着我的份多多翻找！祝你未来好运亨「桶」。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, as a fellow enthusiast, you must take on my share of the trashcan diving, too! May you enjoy your wildest fan-trash-ies!

t_ch: 我亲爱的助手{NICKNAME}！如果你还想补拍，可以随时来找我。
t_en: My beloved assistant, {NICKNAME}! If you want to retake any pictures, you can come and find me any time.

t_ch: 开始今日拍摄。
t_en: Let's begin today's shoot.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 好吧，如果还需要补拍，欢迎随时过来。
t_en: Alright. If you need to retake any pictures, you can come back anytime.

t_ch: 我亲爱的助手{NICKNAME}！你是来帮我搜集数据的么？偶尔也好好休息休息，你也要保重身体啊。别像我这样总胃疼。
t_en: My beloved assistant, {NICKNAME}! Are you here to help me collect data? It's important to get some rest every now and then. You have to take care of your health, you know? You don't want to end up with a stomach ache like me.

t_ch: 开始今日拍摄。
t_en: Let's begin today's shoot.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 好吧，如果还需要补拍，欢迎随时过来。
t_en: Alright. If you need to retake any pictures, you can come back anytime.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，好久不见。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, long time no see!

t_ch: 最近过得如何？希望你们这一路上没遇到什么太大的麻烦。
t_en: How have you been lately? I hope you haven't run into too much trouble on your path.

t_ch: 差点交代在梦里。
t_en: I almost met my end in a dream.

t_ch: 啊…是和那个叫「匹诺康尼」的地方有关吗？我从驻留在下层区的公司员工那里听说了，你们为了解放那颗「监狱星」贡献了很多力量，就像你们为贝洛伯格做的那样。
t_en: Does that have to do with... that place called Penacony? I've heard about it from IPC workers stationed in the Underworld. I hear you all contributed a lot to liberating that "prison planet," much as you did for Belobog.

t_ch: 在履行星穹列车的助人之道时，各位也要注意自己的身体健康…这是我身为医生能给出的唯一建议。
t_en: It is important for you all to take care of your own health as you carry out your mission of helping others as passengers of the Astral Express... This is the only suggestion I can give you as a doctor.

t_ch: 差点…染上…魔阴身。
t_en: I was almost... stricken... with mara.

t_ch: 魔阴身…听上去像是一种很难对付的疾病呢。幸好你现在还能完好地站在我的面前，这说明你们克服了那些困难，对吧？
t_en: Mara-struck... That sounds like a hard sickness to handle. Fortunately, you stand before me today in perfect health. That must mean that you overcame these struggles, correct?

t_ch: 觉得累了就要好好休息，得了病就要按照医嘱吃药。虽然我没有去到过那么多的世界，但我相信这些小小的日常道理不论在哪里都适用。照顾好自己，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: If you feel tired, you must rest properly. If you fall ill, you must take the medicines your doctor recommends. Though I may not have been to many worlds, I imagine these simple truths are applicable anywhere. Take care of yourself, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 星穹列车会撞飞所有麻烦！
t_en: The Astral Express blasts away all troubles!

t_ch: 呵呵…没想到星穹列车还有这种用途，你们追随的那位「开拓」星神想得还真周到。
t_en: Haha... I never imagined that the Astral Express had such a function. This Aeon of Trailblaze that you follow truly has every possibility covered.

t_ch: 言归正传吧，星穹列车来去匆匆，留给咱们共处的时间相当宝贵。这几天，贝洛伯格发生了一件怪事……
t_en: Allow me to get back to my original point. The Astral Express is always on the move and any time you and I can meet is precious. Something strange has been happening in Belobog these past few days...

t_ch: 不少居民反映，他们在被神秘的黑影追逐，甚至有些还被抓伤了。光是昨天一天，我这里就收治了十多个被袭击者，手臂上净是大大小小的血痕。
t_en: Many residents have said that they were being pursued by a dark shadow. Some were even scratched and clawed by it. Yesterday alone I treated about a dozen victims with scratches all over their arms.

t_ch: 上上下下，大家对此事都没有头绪。直到今天，一位「新朋友」找上我。他称可以解决这一切，但前提是要找到「你」……
t_en: Not a single one of them has any idea about what this thing is — Until today when a "new friend" came to see me. He said he could solve all of this, but we must find "you" first...

t_ch: 哈，形态，变换！！！
t_en: Hah, transform!!!

t_ch: 这又是哪个奇异的梦吗？你下意识掐了一把自己的手臂，但垃圾桶仍站立于你面前。果然人无法从现实中苏醒……
t_en: Is this another strange dream of some kind? Without realizing it, you find yourself pinching your arm, but the trashcan is still there standing before you. Looks like there's no way to wake up...

t_ch: 呼…呼…呼……苦练了这么久的出场动作，总算派上用场了。你看，就这一下，嘿，腾空转体七百二十度，需要多么强健的核心力量。你看我这腹肌，多么线条分明！
t_en: Phew... Hoo... Phew... I spent so long practicing that debut pose and I finally got to use it! Look, a <unbreak>720</unbreak>-degree spin jump! Do you know how much core strength that takes? Look how ripped my abs are!

t_ch: 这就是那位「新朋友」——小桶宝力先生，很不可思议，对吧？他委托我寻找一位「爱翻垃圾桶的灰发{F#女子}{M#男子}」，直觉告诉我，他要找的是你。
t_en: This is the "new friend" I mentioned: Mr. Litter Pony. He's really something, right? He asked me to find a "young gray-haired {F#lady}{M#man} who loves trashcans." My instincts told me that must mean you.

t_ch: 你认识我？
t_en: You know me?

t_ch: 都对人家做了那样的事情，还装作不认识？你可真无情～
t_en: Are you really going to pretend that you don't know me after what you did to me? So cruel~

t_ch: 你怎么会在这里？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 很意外吗？我也是不得已，才来到你们的世界。
t_en: Are you shocked? I'm only here in this world because I have no choice.

t_ch: 小桶、宝力？
t_en: Litter... Pony?

t_ch: 什么？！我的秘密，这么快就败露了？确实，这并非我的本名，而是我按照我最爱的动画起的圈名，是六个小桶靠爱与魔法拯救世界的故事。莫非…你也是同好？
t_en: What? My secret got exposed already? Indeed, that is not my real name. It is a pseudonym that I created based on my favorite anime. It's the story of six trashcans that save the world using the powers of love and magic. Are you... also a fan?

t_ch: 我原本的名字叫宝力·浮士德·斯威夫特·波特莱尔·科洛迪。你也可以这么叫我，就是有点浪费时间。
t_en: My real name is Pony Faust Swift Baudelaire Collodi. You can use my real name if you prefer... it just takes up a lot of time.

t_ch: 据小桶宝力先生所说，这些黑影生物与他来自于同个世界……我有个疑问——过去贝洛伯格从未遇上类似的入侵事件，如今它们为何要来这里胡作非为？
t_en: According to Mr. Litter Pony, these shadowy creatures hail from the same world as he does... I have a question. Belobog has never experienced an invasion like this before. Why would they be here causing trouble now?

t_ch: 哎！这群家伙，是一种出了名的…爱交朋友的物种！
t_en: Ah! These folks are a species famous for... wanting to make friends!

t_ch: 还有这种好事？
t_en: Really? There's no catch?

t_ch: 别挨我，我社恐。
t_en: Out of my way. I have social anxiety.

t_ch: 只不过它们天生大脑缺根筋，不懂正常的交友思维，只知道使用武力胁迫他人，又误将对方因恐惧展现出的服从视作「友谊」。我们都被它们嚯嚯了一遍，弄得桶心惶惶的。
t_en: But there's something missing in their brains. They don't know how to make friends normally. All they know is using their brute strength to coerce others and mistaking others' fearful subservience for "friendship." We have been attacked and harassed by them and are scared to the core of our little garbage hearts.

t_ch: 它们在我们那儿声名败坏，于是又想着跑来现实宇宙，从人类这里捞些新朋友。据我所知，它们计划的第一步是学习人类的游戏——那些追逐，实际上是在学你们玩捉迷藏呢！
t_en: They have a terrible reputation in my world, so they decided to come to the real universe and grab some human friends. As far as I know, the first step of their plan was to learn some human games... These attacks have actually been them learning how to play your game of hide-and-seek!

t_ch: 怎样可以阻止它们？
t_en: How can we stop them?

t_ch: 如果不加以阻止，它们只会继续采用暴力交友方式。到时候可就是血流成河了！
t_en: If we don't stop them, they will just continue to use their violent methods to make friends. If that happens, it will be a bloodbath!

t_ch: 我愿意和它们捉迷藏。
t_en: I'll play hide-and-seek with them.

t_ch: 可不要惯着它们！如果真能靠暴力手段交到朋友，它们就会变本加厉。到时候可就是血流成河了！
t_en: You mustn't indulge them! If they really do make friends using these violent methods, they will only get more brutal. If that happens, it will be a bloodbath!

t_ch: 依我看，只有一个办法——教会它们什么才是真正的友谊，所以……和我一起使用「友谊魔法」，净化它们吧！
t_en: If you ask me, there's only one thing we can do... We have to teach them what true friendship is! So... Use the Magic of Friendship with me to purify them!

t_ch: 这个我擅长。
t_en: This is my specialty.

t_ch: 嗯，不愧是被我选中的孩子。我们的动作得快一些了。
t_en: Hm, just what I expect from my chosen one! We need to hurry up about it.

t_ch: 我拒绝。
t_en: I refuse.

t_ch: 可是…如果你拒绝，你知道你的朋友们会发生什么吗……
t_en: But... If you refuse, do you know what will happen to your friends...?

t_ch: 能不能整点成年人看的节目？
t_en: Can't we do something a bit less childish?

t_ch: 竟然嫌我幼稚…小屁孩才喜欢黑深残，成年人都喜欢爱与魔法！如果你拒绝，你知道你的朋友们会发生什么吗……
t_en: You're calling me childish...? Children are the ones who like the grimdark, brutal, and horrible stories! It's adults who like love and magic! If you say no, do you know what might happen to your friends?

t_ch: 它们正在学习如何给「朋友」过生日。到时候，你的朋友们会被绑在椅子上，被围着唱三万三千三百三十三遍生日快乐歌！天呐，天下还有比这个更残忍的酷刑吗？！
t_en: They're currently learning how to celebrate a friend's birthday. When that time comes, your friends will be tied to a chair, surrounded, and have the Happy Birthday song sung to them <unbreak>33,333</unbreak> times! Is there any crueler fate in this world!?

t_ch: 这个友谊魔法…该怎么用？
t_en: So, how are we supposed to use this... Magic of Friendship?

t_ch: 我来说明——我需要借用你与贝洛伯格的伙伴们结下的深厚友谊，毕竟我…我在这个世界没有什么朋友，哈哈哈！
t_en: Let me explain... I will need to borrow the power of your strong friendship with your Belobog friends. After all... I have no friends in this world, hahaha!

t_ch: 我会感应城中「友谊魔法」最强的地方，派我的分身前往那里。
t_en: I shall react to the place with the strongest Magic of Friendship in the city, and send my avatar to that location.

t_ch: 到时候，你需要获得能够代表这段友谊的「友谊凭证」——成功之后，我便能借用「友谊魔法」，与你一同击退黑影生物！你只要跟着我的分身发出的信号，找到我就好。
t_en: After that, you will need to obtain a Proof of Friendship that represents your friendship... Once you are successful, I will be able to use your Magic of Friendship and defeat the dark shadow creatures alongside you! You just need to follow the signal sent by my clone and find me.

t_ch: 这次也就拜托你了，大英雄。
t_en: I'm counting on you this time as well, great hero.

t_ch: 说好要向贝洛伯格的伙伴借用「友谊魔法」，可你并未瞧见任何「朋友」在场……
t_en: The agreement was that you would borrow the Magic of Friendship from your Belobogian friends, but your "friends" are nowhere to be seen...

t_ch: 哟，我们开始吧！
t_en: Alright, let's get started!

t_ch: 我朋友呢？
t_en: What about my friends?

t_ch: 哒哒——你的好朋友，就是我呀！
t_en: Ta-da! Your friend is me!

t_ch: 看你身旁的垃圾桶——我正是这桶扭曲后，诞生于「夹层世界」的意识体。
t_en: Look at the trashcans next to you... I am a consciousness born in the "Betweenwhere" after this trashcan was distorted.

t_ch: 我本无人问津，一桶蜷缩在角落，默默承受着这个城市吐出的尾气。
t_en: No one ever paid me any mind. I was curled up in a corner as a can, silently at the mercy of the city's exhaust fumes.

t_ch: 只有你愿意翻开我的桶，了解我的内在，我的黑色塑料袋、我的鱼骨、我的苹果核、我的一切……
t_en: Only you were willing to open me up and get familiar with my insides. My black plastic sack, my fish bones, my apple cores, every part of me...

t_ch: 人家从里到外，你比谁都了解，难道还要装作与我不熟悉吗？你这个…{F#渣女}{M#渣男}！
t_en: You know my insides better than anyone. Surely you are not going to stand here and pretend we are no more than strangers, are you? You... {F#heartbreaker}{M#jerk}!

t_ch: 我会对你负起责任的！
t_en: I will take responsibility!

t_ch: 哄人的话，你倒是一套一套的，伤人心的事没少干吧！
t_en: You know how to sweet talk when you need to. You must have broken many hearts in your time!

t_ch: 哎，我也只能睁只眼闭着眼。凑合过吧。
t_en: Ah, guess I have no choice but to let it slide and make do.

t_ch: 我不是我没有，你不要过来！
t_en: I didn't! It wasn't me! Don't come any closer!

t_ch: 不要狡辩了！你翻找垃圾桶时的英姿，我全都看得一清二楚。
t_en: Quit trying to make excuses! I've seen your charming form as you dived into a trashcan up close and personal!

t_ch: 来展示我们的「友谊凭证」吧！快点，来不及解释了，把我头上这盖子打开，使出你平日里奋力翻垃圾的劲头——
t_en: Let's show our Proof of Friendship! There's no time to explain. Open up the lid on my head and unleash that trashcan-diving vigor of yours...

t_ch: （照做）
t_en: (Comply)

t_ch: 你使出一套熟悉得不能再熟悉的连招。
t_en: You unleash a combo that, at this point, you could do in your sleep.

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了{NICKNAME}的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains {NICKNAME}'s Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: （拒绝）
t_en: (Refuse)

t_ch: 小桶宝力由不得你。它打开自己头顶的盖子，跳起来将桶身倒扣在你头上，垃圾倒出撒落满地。
t_en: Litter Pony does not care what you have to say. He flips open his lid and jumps to land upside-down on your head, spilling trash all over the surrounding area.

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了{NICKNAME}的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains {NICKNAME}'s Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 「友谊魔法」不会说谎，我们果然是最好的朋友。
t_en: The Magic of Friendship never lies. I knew we are best friends.

t_ch: 现在，与我一起并肩战斗吧，朋友！让我们一同净化那些顽劣的「夹层生物」！
t_en: Now, my friend, let us battle side-by-side! Let us purify these terrible "Betweenwhere creatures"!

t_ch: （进入战斗）
t_en: (Enter Battle)

t_ch: 你见到了那个熟悉的粉红色身影……
t_en: You catch sight of that familiar pink silhouette...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，快快快，过来帮我看看！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, quick quick, come and help me check something out!

t_ch: 你能看见旁边这个会说话的…垃圾桶吗？他自称什么桶宝，说是来向我寻求「友谊魔法」的。我不会也是童心未泯，看到了什么卡通中的人物吧？
t_en: Can you see this... talking trashcan next to me? They call themselves Pody or something and they keep talking about looking for some Magic of Friendship from me. You don't think my childlike heart is making me imagine some cartoon character, do you?

t_ch: 我叫小桶宝力，不是桶宝，你少说两个字，四月八姑娘！
t_en: My name is Litter Pony, not Pody! You missed out half of my name, Miss April 8th!

t_ch: 唔…你这个人，我再说一遍，我叫三月七！
t_en: Argh, this one... I'll tell you one more time, my name is March 7th!

t_ch: 宝力先生，让你见笑了。
t_en: I'm sorry about her, Mr. Pony.

t_ch: 原来搞了半天，你俩认识呀。
t_en: Oh, so you two actually know each other?

t_ch: 好的，五月九。
t_en: Alright, May 9th.

t_ch: 原来你能听见他说话呀。不过五月九是怎么回事啦。
t_en: So you can hear him talking, too? Wait. Hey! What do you mean "May 9th"?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你俩看起来关系真好。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you two seem to get along really well?

t_ch: 那是当然，咱俩一起什么大风大浪没经历过。「友谊凭证」什么的，也是随便开！说吧，需要本姑娘做什么。
t_en: Of course! We've been through everything together. This Proof of Friendship or whatever is no issue for us! Well, what is it that you want me to do?

t_ch: 呼——给我一点时间…我在这里感受到……一场伟大路途的开始，那便是你们「友谊凭证」的关键。
t_en: Phew... Just a moment... I can sense... It began with a great journey. That was the key to your Proof of Friendship.

t_ch: 哦，那件事啊！{NICKNAME}，你快告诉他——
t_en: Oh, that! {NICKNAME}, tell him about—

t_ch: 这是我们冒险之旅的起点。
t_en: This was how our adventures began.

t_ch: 你娓娓道来，那是与三月七在雅利洛VI号出生入死的一场冒险……
t_en: You begin to talk endlessly about your life-or-death adventure with March 7th on Jarilo VI...

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了三月七的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains March 7th's Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 这是我转生成为复读机的起点。
t_en: This is the story of how I was reborn as The Echoer.

t_ch: 你向小桶宝力讲述三月七赐予你「复读机」之名的那一天，并告诉三月七这对于你人生意义之重大。
t_en: You tell Litter Pony all about the day that March 7th bestowed you with the name of "The Echoer," as well as telling March 7th how significant that day was to your life.

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了三月七的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains March 7th's Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 咦…我怎么变成这幅张牙舞爪的模样了！
t_en: Wh—Why do I look so aggressive and angry?

t_ch: 「友谊魔法」生效后，我的桶身上会浮现你在你朋友心中真实的模样……
t_en: Your Magic of Friendship has been activated. My can will show the true image that your friend holds of you in their heart.

t_ch: …开玩笑的啦！来看看六月十姑娘的实力。
t_en: ...Kidding! Let's see what your true power is like, Miss June <unbreak>10</unbreak>th.

t_ch: 呃啊…这梗还没过啊。
t_en: Eurgh... You're still running with that joke, huh?

t_ch: （进入战斗）
t_en: (Enter Battle)

t_ch: 你看到了吗？我俩的「友谊魔法」，三五下就干掉了那些怪物！
t_en: Did you see that? Our Magic of Friendship wiped out those monsters in seconds!

t_ch: 下次有这种好玩的事，记得再叫上我！
t_en: Next time there's something fun like this going on, you have to let me know!

t_ch: 三月七对刚刚的战斗很满意。
t_en: March 7th seems very satisfied with how the battle went.

t_ch: 你望着眼前这个男人，十分惊讶小桶宝力会找上他——他也算得上是你的「朋友」吗？
t_en: Looking at the man before you, you're shocked that Litter Pony would call him along for this... Would you consider him your "friend"?

t_ch: 哎呀呀，这不是{NICKNAME}大客户嘛！
t_en: Woah, if it isn't {NICKNAME}, my favorite customer!

t_ch: 「友谊凭证」的事，找我可就对了！毕竟我可是你最值得信赖的老桑博啊，是吧？
t_en: You came to the right person for a Proof of Friendship! I'm your trustworthy old Sampo after all, right?

t_ch: 哪只眼睛看出我信任你了？
t_en: Do you really see trust when you look in my eyes?

t_ch: 「桑博」是「信任」的反义词。
t_en: "Sampo" and "trustworthy" have the opposite meaning.

t_ch: 话别说的这么难听嘛，{F#姐们儿}{M#哥们儿}！想开点，友谊的本质不就是利益交换嘛？
t_en: My friend, how can you be so cruel? Think about it. Isn't the essence of friendship just mutual benefit?

t_ch: 我俩的交情，怎么看都是符合标准定义的「朋友」——你出钱，我出力，这难道不是友情最纯粹、最完美的呈现形式吗？
t_en: No matter how you look at it, our relationship meets the standard definition of "friendship" in every way. You pay with money, and I pay with effort. Is this not friendship in its purest, most perfect form?

t_ch: 虽然听起来一点都不纯粹，但我确实在这里感知到了…苦涩与甜蜜参半的友谊。
t_en: I can't say that sounds pure to me, but I still sense... a bittersweet friendship between you.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你觉得这「友谊凭证」要怎么弄到手？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how do you think you would be able to obtain your Proof of Friendship?

t_ch: （努力回忆桑博身上的闪光点）
t_en: (Strain to recall good points on Sampo)

t_ch: 你努力回忆往日与桑博相处的经历…如此说来，他好像的确从银鬃铁卫手中救过你、丹恒和三月七一回。
t_en: You begin thinking back really hard on your past experiences with Sampo... As you do, you realize that there actually was that time when he saved you, Dan Heng, and March 7th from the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了桑博的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains Sampo's Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: （向桑博购买「友谊凭证」）
t_en: (Buy Proof of Friendship from Sampo)

t_ch: 你掏出了一沓信用点，这是你心中最适用于桑博的打交道方式。
t_en: You pull out a stack of credits. In your mind, this is the most appropriate way to interact with Sampo.

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了桑博的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains Sampo's Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 这就对了，欢迎来到大人的世界！
t_en: That's more like it. Welcome to the adults' world!

t_ch: 喏，{NICKNAME}，这就是你要的，成年人能看的东西。
t_en: Here, {NICKNAME}. This is what you wanted — the stuff only adults can see.

t_ch: （进入战斗）
t_en: (Enter Battle)

t_ch: 朋友有难，老桑博一定拔刀相助！看吧，哪怕是到对面当卧底也可以！
t_en: If a friend is in trouble, I will always rush to their aid! See, even if it means infiltrating the enemy and taking them down from the inside!

t_ch: …还真是符合本人性格的战斗方式。
t_en: ...Truly a fighting style fitting for his personality...

t_ch: 一人一桶聊得热火朝天，你很好奇这桶能与铁卫情报官有什么共同语言……
t_en: Seeing this human and trashcan engaged in heated conversation, you can't help but be curious about what common language a trashcan could have with a Silvermane Guard Intelligence Officer.

t_ch: 你们在聊什么？
t_en: What are you two talking about?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，好久不见！欢迎回到贝洛伯格。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, long time no see! Welcome back to Belobog!

t_ch: 刚和宝力大大聊得有些入迷，我俩喜欢的作品很类似，宝力大大还送了我一个签绘…没想到北极圈作品还能吃上热乎的饭，我真是太幸福了！
t_en: I was just having a fascinating conversation with Pony. It turns out that we like a lot of the same works, and he even generously gifted me an autographed painting... I never thought I'd see fresh new works from such an obscure fandom. I'm so happy!

t_ch: 我才是要感谢佩拉小姐愿意吃我的安利。刚刚聊到哪儿了，对了，我在和她讨论「友谊魔法」的事呢。
t_en: I should be the one thanking you for taking my recommendation, Miss Pela. What were we talking about just now? Oh, that's right, we were discussing the Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 曾经有部热门动画，主题就是关于「友谊魔法」拯救世界：和最好的朋友展开激动人心的冒险，还可以拥有华丽漂亮的魔法招式……
t_en: There was once a popular anime that was all about using the Magic of Friendship to save the world: The characters would set out on exciting adventures with their best friends, and could also wield wonderful magical techniques...

t_ch: 我还给这动画…写过一点同人文，没想到这种魔法竟然真的存在！
t_en: I actually... wrote a few fanfics about this anime, too. I never imagined that this kind of magic really existed!

t_ch: 如果可以的话，我也想…体验一下。请务必告诉我，怎样才能使出「友谊魔法」！
t_en: If it's possible, I would love the chance to... experience this for myself. Please tell me how I can use this Magic of Friendship!

t_ch: 佩拉小姐的话，一定没问题的！检验佩拉小姐「友谊凭证」的时间到了，少女应该怎么做呢——
t_en: That will not be a problem for you, Miss Pela! It's time to test Miss Pela's Proof of Friendship. What do you think a girl like her should do...?

t_ch: 与朋友分享自己写的同人文。
t_en: Share her fanfics with her friends.

t_ch: 佩拉害羞地展示了自己主笔的同人文，那是与好朋友玲可一同成为贝洛伯格魔法少女的故事。
t_en: Pela shyly shows a fanfic she wrote — it's a story of her and her good friend Lynx becoming magical girls of Belobog together.

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了佩拉的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains Pela's Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 拉上朋友成为魔法少女！
t_en: Become a magical girl with her friends!

t_ch: 佩拉害羞地展示了自己主笔的同人文——那是与好朋友玲可一起成为贝洛伯格魔法少女的故事。
t_en: Pela shyly shows a fanfic she wrote — it's a story of her and her good friend Lynx becoming magical girls of Belobog together.

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了佩拉的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains Pela's Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 这样就可以了吗？
t_en: Is that all I needed to do?

t_ch: 这就是…我的「友谊魔法」？
t_en: This is my... Magic of Friendship?

t_ch: 没错！现在，佩拉小姐，你也是真正的魔法少女了——赶快投入实战，和我一起打败那些「夹层入侵者」吧！
t_en: That's right! Now, you are a true magical girl, Miss Pela! It's time for you to join the real battlefield and defeat these "Betweenwhere intruders" by my side!

t_ch: （进入战斗）
t_en: (Enter Battle)

t_ch: 我的「友谊魔法」招式，好像没有想象中的可爱。不知道玲宝的会是怎么样的。
t_en: My Magic of Friendship attack isn't as cute as I imagined it would be... I wonder what Lynxy's would be like...

t_ch: （佩拉有些沮丧。）
t_en: (Pela seems a little disheartened.)

t_ch: 一人一桶站在孤儿院门口的高台眺望远方，有说有笑。
t_en: You see a person and a trashcan standing on the platform in front of the orphanage, laughing and talking as they stare into the distance.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，好久不见。请代我向星穹列车的各位问好。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, it's been a while! Please say hello to the Express Crew for me when you see them.

t_ch: 小桶宝力先生向我说明了情况，所幸最近发生的怪事有了破局的办法。看来，又要麻烦你了。
t_en: Mr. Litter Pony has explained the situation to me. Fortunately, it seems there is a solution to all these recent strange happenings. Looks like we're counting on you once more.

t_ch: 举手之劳。
t_en: Piece of cake.

t_ch: 还有宝力先生，也让你费心了…谢谢你能把贝洛伯格的安危放在心上。
t_en: I'd like to thank you for all your effort too, Mr. Pony... Thank you for thinking of Belobog's safety.

t_ch: 我被逼的。
t_en: I'm being forced to do this.

t_ch: 但你现在选择站在这里，这证明你还是有心为贝洛伯格解决这个麻烦的，对吧？
t_en: But the fact that you have chosen to be here now proves that you are still willing to help Belobog resolve this problem, wouldn't you agree?

t_ch: 朋友，做好事要留名！大方承认吧，你根本就是个责任心爆棚的人！
t_en: My friend, it is okay to take credit for good deeds! Just admit that you are the kind of person that is extremely concerned for others!

t_ch: 提到「友谊魔法」，回忆一下涌上心头，不知不觉就和宝力先生说了好多。可能有些啰嗦，不好意思。
t_en: When you mentioned the Magic of Friendship, so many memories came flooding back to me. Before I knew it, I had been talking non-stop for ages with Mr. Pony. Sorry for getting carried away and rambling.

t_ch: 我很荣幸能听到这些…这位优雅的小姐，结识了志同道合的新朋友，又与一位旧友重逢。
t_en: I was honored that you would share all this with me... This elegant young lady has made a new, like-minded friend and has been able to reunite with an old one, too.

t_ch: 每一段经历，都是弥足珍贵的「友谊凭证」。在与各位的相处中，我学会了很多。
t_en: Every experience is truly a Proof of Friendship that should be treasured. I have learned a lot through meeting all of you.

t_ch: 学会了如何齐心协力。
t_en: Like how to unite our efforts with our hearts aligned.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅笑了笑，开始回忆你们同心协力、共同拯救贝洛伯格的旅程……
t_en: Bronya laughs softly and begins to reminisce about how your hearts were aligned and your efforts were united on your journey to save Belobog together...

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了布洛妮娅的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains Bronya's Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 学会了如何说粗鄙之语。
t_en: Like how to swear in foul language.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅笑了笑，讲起了希儿孜孜不倦教导她「粗鄙之语」的经历……
t_en: Bronya laughs softly and begins to talk about Seele's tireless efforts to teach her "foul language"...

t_ch: 一阵金光闪过，小桶宝力获得了布洛妮娅的「友谊魔法」。
t_en: With a bright flash of light, Litter Pony obtains Bronya's Magic of Friendship.

t_ch: 小桶宝力先生身上画的…是我吗？
t_en: This picture on Mr. Litter Pony's body... is that me?

t_ch: 没错，我也会继承一些你作战的风格。
t_en: That's right. I will also inherit part of your combat style.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，准备好了吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you ready?

t_ch: （进入战斗）
t_en: (Enter Battle)
